definition: The labor force growth channel that contributed ~25-40% of 1946-1980 deleveraging is structurally impaired by demographics. Labor force growth declined from ~1.5%/year to ~0.3%/year (75-80% decline). Deleveraging via growth alone would take 3-5x longer than the post-WWII period.
evidence: Labor force growth contributed ~25-40% of 1946-1980 deleveraging
Labor force growth has fallen from ~1.5%/year to ~0.3%/year
75-80% decline in the growth channel is arithmetically verifiable
Directional constraint survived all debate scrutiny
open_questions: Can inflation do the additional work (pushing toward 5-6%)?
How much can AI-driven productivity realistically contribute?
What is the realistic timeline for deleveraging under current demographic conditions?
